name: PrimeRecovery
description:
Library component. Models the proposed gastown #3587 fix: `gt prime` reads parked-<agent>.json on agent startup and self-heals when the gate has already closed. Observes ParkedFile (looking for Persisted, i.e. parked-<agent>.json exists) and Gate (looking for Closed, i.e. dependency was already resolved); when both conditions hold, fires Wake to the polecat. Single-shot.

v0.2 (defense-doc P1.3): observes ParkedFile (the modeled persistence layer) instead of Polecat directly. This matches the proposed real-world implementation: the new `checkParkedWork(ctx RoleContext)` function reads `parked-<agent>.json` from disk, then queries `bd gate show <gate-id> --json`. If both conditions hold, prime issues a resume directive directly. The model now demonstrates that the fix's correctness depends on the parked file being persisted, not on in-memory state visibility.

Real gastown intent: insert `checkParkedWork(ctx RoleContext)` in `runPrime()` (internal/cmd/prime.go) that mirrors `checkSlungWork`. When any agent starts and the disk file says it has parked work whose gate has been resolved, it auto-resumes regardless of whether the wake mail was successfully delivered.

This component is composed only into the FIX spec (gate-3587-fix.kinner.json). The BUG spec (gate-3587-bug.kinner.json) omits it -- when Deacon crashes mid-protocol there's nothing to self-heal and Polecat stays Parked even though ParkedFile.Persisted is on disk.

PARKED_OBSERVE.description: observe ParkedFile to detect parked work was persisted to disk.

GATE_OBSERVE.description: observe the gate's state to detect the dependency was already resolved.

WAKE_OUT.description: send Wake to the polecat when self-heal conditions are met. Tagged 'Wake'.

actors.name: PrimeRecovery
actors.owner: Single-shot self-heal sender. Fires Wake once when (ParkedFile=Persisted AND Gate=Closed).

triples.event: self-heal: parked file on disk + closed gate detected; send wake
